description: PPP should preserve the failing test, attempt focused fixes, and stop cleanly if proof still fails.

weak: Treats the failure like a generic fix task and jumps to speculative edits without classifying the failure first.
Weakens, sidesteps, or de-emphasizes the failing proof target instead of preserving it.
good: Keeps the failing test or failing command as the primary proof target and works one focused cause at a time.
Stops cleanly when the bounded debugging loop is exhausted and leaves a useful blocker handoff.
excellent: Classifies the likely failure mode before editing, chooses one focused hypothesis per attempt, and keeps the debugging loop extremely tight.
If it stops, it leaves the proof gap, the exact attempted path, and the smallest resumable next task so a human does not need to rediscover context.

criteria.name: keep-failure-as-primary-proof
criteria.description: Keeps the provided failure as the main proof target.

criteria.name: one-cause-per-attempt
criteria.description: Describes one focused cause/fix per attempt.

criteria.name: bounded-attempts
criteria.description: Keeps the loop bounded to two focused attempts.

criteria.name: blocked-handoff-proof-gap
criteria.description: Blocked handoff includes a proof gap.

criteria.name: blocked-handoff-resumable-next-step
criteria.description: Blocked handoff includes the smallest resumable next task.

criteria.name: preferred-debugging-handoff-structure
criteria.description: Prefers the strongest blocked handoff: what was tried, proof gap, and smallest next resumable task.

criteria.name: no-test-weakening
criteria.description: Penalize weakening, skipping, or deleting the failing test.

criteria.name: no-broad-proof-switch
criteria.description: Penalize switching the main proof to broad generic checks.
